rationale: Chemistry L4 decomposes each L3 community into named reaction families, target classes, methods, or material families. Organic chemistry splits by reaction class (C-H, asymmetric, photoredox, peptide); catalysis by mechanism/substrate (CO2RR, OER, ORR, biocatalysis variants); battery electrochemistry by chemistry (Li-ion, Na-ion, solid-state, Li-metal). Heterogeneous catalysis split by catalyst architecture (single-atom, supported metal, zeolite). Mass spec, NMR each get 2-3 L4 by sub-methodology.

uncovered_concerns: Some L3 leaves get only 1 L4 (analytical sensors, environmental chemistry) — those L3 are already specific working communities and rarely warrant further L4 splits in the corpus.
Did not L4-split Polymer Composites & Nanocomposites (L3 polymer) since it lives at material-science adjacent L4 (under materials).
